course.title: JavaScript Basics
course.description: Learn the programming language of the web.

chapter.title: Getting Started with JS
chapter.description: Introduction to JavaScript syntax and console logging.

lessons.title: Hello, Console!
lessons.explanation:
JavaScript is the programming language that powers the interactive elements on websites. 

To see what your code is doing, you can print messages to the console using `console.log()`.
lessons.analogy: If HTML is the skeleton and CSS is the skin, JavaScript is the muscle that makes everything move.
best_practices: End your statements with a semicolon (`;`), although modern JavaScript can often figure it out without them.

exercises.statement: Write a JavaScript command to print 'I love JS!' to the console.
